(November 11, 1934 – January 9, 2021) was an American film and television actor who appeared on soap operas, including *[General Hospital](/wiki/General_Hospital "General Hospital")*, *[Sunset Beach](/wiki/Sunset_Beach_(TV_series) "Sunset Beach (TV series)")*, and *[Passions](/wiki/Passions "Passions")*.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actor (1934–2021)

Not to be confused with [John C. Reilly](/wiki/John_C._Reilly "John C. Reilly").

**John Henry Matthew Reilly** (November 11, 1934 – January 9, 2021) was an American film and television actor who appeared on soap operas, including *[General Hospital](/wiki/General_Hospital "General Hospital")*, *[Sunset Beach](/wiki/Sunset_Beach_(TV_series) "Sunset Beach (TV series)")*, and *[Passions](/wiki/Passions "Passions")*.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Reilly_(actor,_born_1934)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Born in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois"), Reilly began acting in the mid-1960s with guest starring roles on *[Death Valley Days](/wiki/Death_Valley_Days "Death Valley Days")*, *[Apple's Way](/wiki/Apple%27s_Way "Apple's Way")* and *[Gunsmoke](/wiki/Gunsmoke "Gunsmoke")*. In 1974, he replaced John Colenback as [Dr. Dan Stewart](/wiki/Dan_Stewart_(As_the_World_Turns) "Dan Stewart (As the World Turns)") on *[As the World Turns](/wiki/As_the_World_Turns "As the World Turns")*. After a two and a half year run, he left *As the World Turns* in September 1976 and was replaced by his predecessor John Colenback. Airing March 12, 1977, he appeared on, The Mary Tyler Moore Show, in the episode, Lou Dates Mary. He also made two guest appearances on *[The Bionic Woman](/wiki/The_Bionic_Woman "The Bionic Woman")* in 1977 and 1978, and had roles in the 1978 television movies *Lassie: A New Beginning* and *Secrets of Three Hungry Wives*. In addition to television, Reilly also appeared in feature films including *[The Main Event](/wiki/The_Main_Event_(1979_film) "The Main Event (1979 film)")* (1979) and *[Gorp](/wiki/Gorp_(film) "Gorp (film)")* (1980).

In 1983, Reilly had a recurring role on nighttime soap opera *[Dallas](/wiki/Dallas_(TV_series) "Dallas (TV series)")*. The following year, he was cast as WSB agent [Sean Donely](/wiki/Sean_Donely "Sean Donely") on *General Hospital*. He stayed with the series until November 1994 when he opted not to renew his contract because of a pay-out from then-executive producer Wendy Riche. His character was written out of the show in February 1995, when Sean and his on screen wife Tiffany left town. He returned to daytime television as Del Douglas on *[Sunset Beach](/wiki/Sunset_Beach_(TV_series) "Sunset Beach (TV series)")* for a short-term guest role during the show's first six episodes, and then later returned on various occasions during 1997–1999.

Reilly played Kelly Taylor's oft-absentee father, Bill, in several episodes of *Beverly Hills, 90210*. The character left the canvas when Bill Taylor was arrested and sent to jail, following a reconciliation with his daughter.

On January 21, 2005, Reilly took over the role of [Alistair Crane](/wiki/Alistair_Crane "Alistair Crane") on the [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") soap opera *[Passions](/wiki/Passions "Passions")*. He took over the role from [David Bailey](/wiki/David_Bailey_(actor) "David Bailey (actor)") after Bailey died in a pool accident in November 2004. In July 2006, Reilly was dropped from the show as a result of budget cuts. He returned to the role in August 2007 as a recurring character and remained with the show until his character was killed off in May 2008.

On October 21, 2008, Reilly reprised the role of [Sean Donely](/wiki/Sean_Donely "Sean Donely") on *[General Hospital: Night Shift](/wiki/General_Hospital:_Night_Shift "General Hospital: Night Shift")* for the second-season finale. On August 9, 2013, Reilly reprised the role of [Sean Donely](/wiki/Sean_Donely "Sean Donely") on *[General Hospital](/wiki/General_Hospital "General Hospital")* for the 50th anniversary year.

## Personal life and death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Reilly_(actor,_born_1934)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life and death")]

Reilly married Lily-Beth "Liz" Janred (born 1947 in [Halmstad](/wiki/Halmstad "Halmstad"), Sweden) in 1981.[[3]](#cite_note-3) They had five daughters, including [Caitlin](/wiki/Caitlin_Reilly "Caitlin Reilly"), who is also an actress and a social media personality.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Reilly died on January 9, 2021, after living with [Alzheimer's disease](/wiki/Alzheimer%27s_disease "Alzheimer's disease") for many years.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) The cause of death was a heart attack.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

| 2007–2008 | *[Passions](/wiki/Passions "Passions")* | [Alistair Crane](/wiki/Alistair_Crane "Alistair Crane") (#3) | Recurring cast Member |
| 2008 | *[General Hospital: Night Shift](/wiki/General_Hospital:_Night_Shift "General Hospital: Night Shift")* | [Sean Donely](/wiki/Sean_Donely "Sean Donely") | Guest Star |
| 2012 | *[The Bay](/wiki/The_Bay_(web_series) "The Bay (web series)")* | Mortimer | 4 episodes |
